evals.prompt: I'm leading a product team and we need to decide whether to keep our current single-service setup or split one area into event-driven flow. We have reliability complaints during peak usage, two engineers with distributed-systems experience, and a six-week release target. Create a planning document that clearly frames the problem, compares options, and explains trade-offs with verification checkpoints.
evals.expected_output: Method-driven planning output with framing, decision criteria, trade-off rationale, and measurable checkpoints.

evals.prompt: Customers hit the same billing failure every Monday morning and teams blame each other. I need a root-cause-first plan that includes stakeholder alignment, execution sequencing, and adaptation signals so repeat incidents drop within one month.
evals.expected_output: Root-cause-focused plan with explicit diagnosis methods, alignment approach, risk controls, and progress signals.

evals.prompt: I run a community mentorship program with limited volunteers. We need fair participant prioritization, session scheduling, and burnout prevention. Build a non-technical plan that still uses strong decision quality methods and clear communication guidance.
evals.expected_output: Non-technical yet rigorous plan with method-based prioritization, sequencing, communication, and review loops.
